title: senecio tweediei
intro: Senecio tweediei, also known as Woolly Groundsel, is a perennial plant native to New Zealand, characterized by its woolly leaves and small yellow daisy-like flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Senecio tweediei include its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help to ease coughs and congestion, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Senecio tweediei typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves and flowers, which can be consumed as teas or used in topical applications for localized relief.
